data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia thanks Kevin Scott for his service as a member of our company board and the important role he has played in our formation.
data.content:
Public Skills Australia would like to acknowledge the service of Kevin Scott. Our May Board session marked Kevin's final meeting as a member of the Public Skills Australia company board.

Since our formation in 2023, Kevin has been a kind and insightful steward of Public Skills Australia.
In addition to serving on our company board, Kevin is also the outgoing Chair of our Government Subcommittee, and a member of our Audit Finance and Risk Committee.

As an organisation that has rapidly grown and scaled, Kevin has played an active role in guiding our people and development, helping us grow from a nascent company to the Public Skills Australia we are today. It has been a privilege for our team to work with and learn from Kevin over these formative years. 

Our community has been celebrating Kevin's service to Public Skills Australia on our LinkedIn page. If you are a LinkedIn user and would like to share your congratulations with Kevin, we invite you to join us to share your message and read more from our community here[:](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/publicskillsaustralia_please-join-us-in-celebrating-the-contribution-activity-7330474959414169602-W_0R)
https://www.linkedin.com/posts/publicskillsaustralia_please-join-us-in-celebrating-the-contribution-activity-7330474959414169602-W_0R

[![](https://psa-directus-prod.azurewebsites.net/assets/0a952a04-c41b-4a00-9c9b-629ce0c29678)](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/publicskillsaustralia_please-join-us-in-celebrating-the-contribution-activity-7330474959414169602-W_0R)

Thank you, Kevin Scott.
data.name: Thank you, Kevin Scott

data.shortDescription: Government Investigation: The Review of the Government Investigations Qualifications will see the update of several qualifications aligned to current job role requirements.
data.content:
### What is a Technical Committee
Members of a Technical Committee provide guidance and oversight on the development, review, and maintenance of training packages and qualifications. Their role is to ensure that the training content remains relevant, up-to-date, and aligned with current industry standards and workforce needs. 

### Key roles of the Technical Committee
- Provide advice on the purpose of a qualification and application of the principles
- Provide advice on training design, delivery, assessment and implementation
- Provide guidance on the Australian Qualifications Framework levels
- Determine impacts on learners and identify implementation issues
- Identify additional stakeholders to include in consultation where necessary

### Who we are seeking
We are seeking Technical Committee representatives with one or more of the following attributes and experience.
- Subject matter expertise in investigatory roles within the public sector
- Educational experience in training individuals for investigatory roles
- State or territory government agencies representatives with relevant expertise
- Represent and understand the requirements of Registered Training Organisations
- Industry Regulatory Representatives
- Union representatives 

### Requirements
- Attend approximately **5 meetings** between **November 2025 to April 2026** to provide advice in the development of training products (dates to be confirmed)
- Consult within your organisation to provide insights on relevant internal policies and standard operating procedures

We request that your nomination for Technical Committee membership is submitted to Public Skills Australia by close of business **Friday 3 October 2025**.

To submit your nomination for the Technical Committee, please complete the [Expression of Interest](https://forms.office.com/r/gvqzW2dfsG).

If you have any questions regarding the requirements of the Technical Committee membership or about the development process, please reach out to: 
Training Product Development team: [tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au](mailto:tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au)
data.name: Expression of Interest: Government Investigations Qualifications Review Technical Committee

data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is delivering an important initiative to review and update the Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Training Qualifications. Join us for for consultative workshops in Auslan and English.
data.content: ## Consultative workshops conducted in Auslan and English Public Skills Australia is delivering an important initiative to review and update the Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Training Qualifications. This critical project aims to ensure these qualifications meet the evolving needs of students, professionals, and all users of Auslan, both now and into the future. We are inviting key stakeholders, members of the Deaf and Deafblind communities, representatives from Deaf advocacy organisations, industry professionals, and service providers to participate in a Consultative Workshop. This workshop will provide a collaborative environment, bringing together representatives from government and non-government organisations alongside community members. Together, we will identify challenges within the training and interpreting industry, explore barriers, and uncover opportunities for improvement. Your insights and expertise will play a vital role in shaping meaningful updates to the qualifications, ensuring they address the needs of the community and the professional industry. ### Workshop Agenda - Current State of Auslan Interpreting in Australia & Global Best Practices. - Perspectives on Current Qualifications. - Identifying Areas for Improvement. - Building a Pathway for Action. The workshop will be held in person, with refreshments provided, including morning tea and lunch for all attendees. To ensure accessibility and inclusivity, Auslan Interpreters will be available to support the discussions. ### Why Participate? - Contribute your experiences and ideas to drive meaningful, positive change. - Collaborate with industry experts, government officials, and like-minded individuals. - Play a key role in strengthening interpreting and translating practices in Australia. ### Who Should Attend? We are running two workshop streams, a Community Workshop stream and an Education Workshop stream. For the Community Workshop stream we invite: - Members of the Deaf and Deafblind communities with lived experience. - Individuals who use Auslan in their personal or professional lives. - Representatives from government and non-government organisations, Deaf advocacy groups, and service providers. For the Education Workshop stream, we invite: - Education providers, educators, trainers, and education support staff. - Graduates of Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating programs. - Current students enrolled in these qualifications. - Representatives from government and non-government organisations. If you are passionate about shaping the future of the Auslan qualifications, we strongly encourage you to participate in this consultative workshop. ### Next Steps To ensure inclusion and representation we are seeking expressions of interest to participate in this workshop. We invite you to register your interest for our workshops online and in your State or territory. | Location | Date | Workshop | | ------ | ------ | ------ | | Melbourne | Monday May 19, 2025 | Community Workshop | | Melbourne | Tuesday May 20, 2025 | Education Workshop | | Perth | Monday May 26, 2025 | Community Workshop | | Perth | Tuesday May 27, 2025 | Education Workshop | | Adelaide | Thursday May 29, 2025 | Community and Education (combined) Workshop | | Brisbane | Wednesday June 4, 2025 | Community Workshop | | Brisbane | Thursday June 5, 2025 | Education Workshop | | Sydney | Wednesday June 11, 2025 | Community Workshop | | Sydney | Thursday June 12, 2025 | Education Workshop | Don't see your city? We will also be running online workshops. If you would like to join us online, please email us to register your interest and we'll let you know when these are available to join. Your expertise is highly valued, and we look forward to working with you to build a stronger, more inclusive future for the Auslan interpreting and translating community. ### Contact Us For further information, please contact us at: 📧 auslanproject@publicskillsaustralia.org.au Thank you for your commitment to this important initiative.
data.name: Let's Shape the Future of Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Qualifications Together

data.shortDescription: The Department of Employment and Workplace Relations has recently revised the Standards for Registered Training Organisations.
data.content:
The Department of Employment and Workplace Relations have recently revised the Standards for Registered Training Organisations (RTOs). The revised Standards will come into full regulatory effect from **1 July 2025.**

These changes have been designed to increase clarity for RTOs and regulators to promote quality outcomes for candidates and employers.

The revisions to the Standards for RTOs include a change of structure to help provide the VET sector with clear guidance on the requirements to deliver nationally recognised training and comprise of three elements:

- The Outcome Standards
- The Compliance Requirements
- The Credential Policy

Each of the policies above include changes that will affect requirements to RTOs, and trainers and assessors.

Changes that may be relevant for our industries include changes to **The Credential Policy**.

This includes the recognition of new and superseded credentials for the training and assessment of training package products.

These include:

- The recognition of those who hold TAE40110 Certificate IV in Training and Assessment, without the requirement additional units of competency.
- The recognition of the credentials of secondary school teachers to perform certain roles within the VET sector.
- Additional guidance on the use and function of TAE Skill Sets for Volunteer Trainers and/or assessors.

You can find more information on the revised Standards below:

[Revisions to the Standards for Registered Training Organisations - Department of Employment and Workplace Relations, Australian Government](https://www.dewr.gov.au/standards-for-rtos)

To support RTOs with the transition to the revised Standards, the Australian Skills Quality Authority (ASQA) have developed a range of resources that can be found below:

[Preparing for the revised Standards | Australian Skills Quality Authority (ASQA)](https://www.asqa.gov.au/how-we-regulate/revised-standards-rtos/preparing-revised-standards)
data.name: Update to RTO Standards

data.shortDescription: Procurement and Contracting: The updated Procurement and Contracting qualifications will incorporate contemporary best practices as outlined in the 2024 Commonwealth Procurement Rules and align with current industry requirements.
data.content:
### What is a Technical Committee
Members of a Technical Committee provide guidance and oversight on the development, review, and maintenance of training packages and qualifications. Their role is to ensure that the training content remains relevant, up-to-date, and aligned with current industry standards and workforce needs.

### Key roles of the Technical Committee
- Provide advice on the purpose of a qualification and application of the principles.
- Provide advice on training design, delivery, assessment and implementation.
- Provide guidance on the Australian Qualification Framework levels.
- Determine impacts on learners and identify implementation issues.
- Identify additional stakeholders to include in consultation where necessary.

### Who we are seeking
We are seeking Technical Committee representatives with one or more of the following attributes and experience:
- Subject matter expertise in procurement and contracting roles with public sector.
- Educational experience in training individuals for procurement and contracting roles.
- State or territory government agencies representatives with relevant expertise.
- Represent and understand the requirements of Registered Training Organisations.
- Industry Regulatory Representatives.
- Union representatives

### Requirements
- Attend approximately **5 meetings** between **November 2025 to April 2026** to develop training products (dates to be confirmed).
- Consult within your organisation to provide insights on relevant internal policies and standard operating procedures.

We request that your nomination for Technical Committee membership is submitted to Public Skills Australia by close of business **Friday 3 October 2025**.

To submit your nomination for the Technical Committee, please complete the [Expression of Interest](https://forms.office.com/r/KMGCxNyEuX).

If you have any questions regarding the requirements of the Technical Committee membership or about the development process, please reach out to:

Training Product Development team: [tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au](mailto:tpd@publicskillsaustralia.org.au)
data.name: Expression of Interest: Procurement and Contracting Qualifications Review Technical Committee

data.shortDescription: Public Skills Australia is delivering an important initiative to review and update the Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Training Qualifications. Following in-person workshops around Australia, join us for online consultative workshops in Auslan and English.
data.content:
### Online Consultative workshops in Auslan and English 

Public Skills Australia is delivering an important initiative to review and update the Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating Training Qualifications. This critical project aims to ensure these qualifications meet the evolving needs of students, professionals, and all users of Auslan, both now and into the future. 

We are inviting key stakeholders including members of the Deaf and Deafblind communities, representatives from Deaf advocacy organisations, industry professionals, and service providers to participate in a Consultative Workshop. This workshop will provide a collaborative environment, bringing together representatives from government and non-government organisations alongside community members. 

Together, we will identify challenges within the training and interpreting industry, explore barriers, and uncover opportunities for improvement. Your insights and expertise will play a vital role in shaping meaningful updates to the qualifications, ensuring they address the needs of the community and the professional industry. 

The online consultation workshops are the digital version of the in-person workshops and provide an opportunity for participation for all stakeholders who were unable to join us for our in-person workshops. Multiple Auslan interpreters will be present to support delivery of these online workshops. Zoom will be the application we will use to connect. 

### Workshop Agenda 

- Current State of Auslan Interpreting in Australia & Global Best Practices. 
- Perspectives on Current Qualifications. 
- Identifying Areas for Improvement. 
- Building a Pathway for Action. 

The workshop will be held in person, with refreshments provided, including morning tea and lunch for all attendees. To ensure accessibility and inclusivity, Auslan Interpreters will be available to support the discussions. 

### Why Participate? 

- Contribute your experiences and ideas to drive meaningful, positive change. 
- Collaborate with industry experts, government officials, and like-minded individuals. 
- Play a key role in strengthening interpreting and translating practices in Australia. 

### Who Should Attend? 

We welcome all people with a connection to the Auslan training package to attend. People who may have a particular interest in participating include: 
- Members of the Deaf and Deafblind communities with lived experience. 
- Individuals who use Auslan in their personal or professional lives. 
- Representatives from government and non-government organisations, Deaf advocacy groups, and service providers. 
- Education providers, educators, trainers, and education support staff. 
- Graduates of Auslan, Interpreting, and Translating programs. 
- Current students enrolled in these qualifications. 
- Representatives from government and non-government organisations. 

If you are passionate about shaping the future of the Auslan qualifications, we strongly encourage you to participate in this consultative workshop. 

### Next Steps 

Online workshops will be held **Tuesday 24 June**, 10am – 12pm, **Wednesday 25 June** 2pm-4pm, and **Wednesday 25 June** 7pm – 9pm. Times are given in AEST/GMT+10 as we are based in Melbourne, though you are welcome to join us from wherever you are based. 

To join an online workshop, please register here: https://forms.office.com/r/Gd1A90Q8Ki 

If you are unable to join one of our online workshops but would still be open to providing feedback, we welcome your contribution here: 
https://forms.office.com/r/fnxXdZz7r1 

Your expertise is highly valued, and we look forward to working with you to build a stronger, more inclusive future for the Auslan interpreting and translating community. 

### Contact Us 

For further information, please contact us at: 

📧 auslanproject@publicskillsaustralia.org.au 

Thank you for your commitment to this important initiative.
data.name: Register Now for Auslan Online Consultative Workshops
